title: Racosperma xiphophyllum
intro: Racosperma xiphophyllum, commonly known as Queensland Silver Wattle, is a deciduous shrub native to the subtropical regions of eastern Australia. Traditionally used in Indigenous Australian medicine, the plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions, such as wound healing and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Racosperma xiphophyllum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused as a tincture, and it is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments.
taxonomy: Racosperma xiphophyllum is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Australia. The plant is also known as Acacia xiphophylla. Racosperma xiphophyllum is a member of the subgenus Phyllodinae. It is closely related to other species within the Acacia genus.
